sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Dolos by TxPipe: Maintaining Cardano's Lightweight Data Node, Year 2, including maintenance and enhancement plus Intersect administration.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of TxPipe as vendor

Deliverables: Part-time maintainer for Dolos over 12 months after the existing contract closes, Essential maintenance of the Dolos codebase including dependency updates, Cardano protocol compatibility, performance improvements, bug fixing, and documentation, Community support including issue triage, review of external contributions, feedback-driven enhancements, and public developer communication, AI-friendly documentation and integration resources for AI-driven development workflows, Milestone-based disbursement controls through Intersect-managed treasury smart contracts
Deadline/expiry: 12 months; exact dates not stated in document

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve and a 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee, for a total withdrawal of 540,750 ADA.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Dolos exposes five query interfaces, including Mini-Blockfrost HTTP, UTxO-RPC gRPC, Mini-Kupo, and an Ouroboros N2C Unix socket compatible with Cardano-cli, Ogmios, and other tooling.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The document states Dolos has 939 commits, 80 releases, 129 stars, 51 forks, and 27 contributors, with a GitHub repository provided for verification.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested work covers essential maintenance, community support, and AI-friendly documentation and integration resources for Dolos.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and was advanced for on-chain Treasury Withdrawal submission.
missing: Independent evidence for: Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes.

Independent evidence for: Dolos exposes five query interfaces, including Mini-Blockfrost HTTP, UTxO-RPC gRPC, Mini-Kupo, and an Ouroboros N2C Unix socket compatible with Cardano-cli, Ogmios, and other tooling.
Independent evidence for: TxPipe is said to have received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals from Funds 9 through 14, with 26 delivered and 4 under development and on schedule.

Independent evidence for: Audit and oversight costs are said to be included in the proposal overhead, with independent oversight through Intersect and technically capable third parties plus reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 420,000 ADA plus a 105,000 ADA contingency reserve and a 15,750 ADA Intersect administration fee, for a total withdrawal of 540,750 ADA." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Dolos is described as a lightweight Cardano data node in Rust that provides efficient chain-data access without the overhead of traditional full nodes." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
